module_mood_contract: grounded competence — 'I can feel when a plan is good before approving it, and I know the move is two reads, not one'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:26 — 'The room harvests everyone's before the lecture names the pattern.' This is a trainer-cohort-management cue authored into student-facing body. It tells the trainer (and implicitly the room) what happens before the lecture fires. In self-study the persona hits it and pauses ('is there a room?'); in cohort it reads as a scripted pause beat. Violates the rule: curriculum body must be about THE WORK (the artifact, the prompt, what the student does next), not COHORT MANAGEMENT (pacing, comparing, sharing).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Cut 'The room harvests everyone's before the lecture names the pattern.' The question itself lands without it — the trainer reads the room and decides what to collect; they don't need the cue in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — name three observable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module-level maintainer block has mood target, push-back moves, watch-fors, and decision points but no leap test naming three observable Monday-morning outcomes the student exhibits on their own codebase. The rule (new 2026-05-03) requires a leap test in the maintainer block; story judge blocks ship on missing or unfalsifiable leap tests.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a 'Leap test' row to the maintainer block naming three verifiable Monday-morning behaviors — e.g., '(1) enters plan mode on any non-trivial task before approving; (2) sends at least one keep-planning-with-feedback push-back before approval; (3) runs a second-pass walk-down and names the pattern it surfaced vs their own read.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch for every forcing function.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The module-level maintainer block has push-back moves and watch-fors (which partially cover failure modes), but no explicit per-phase 'failure mode + escape hatch' rows keyed to the forcing functions in the module body itself (the 'Stop. Don't execute.' beat; the compound-only-if-earned gate; the Key Concepts reading). The rule (new 2026-05-03) requires the maintainer block to name (a) dominant failure under each forcing function and (b) one escape hatch. Note: the exercise-level maintainer blocks (push-back-on-the-plan.md, extract-the-task-shaping-rule.md) DO have these — but the module-file's block is the story judge's primary surface.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a 'Forcing function failure modes' section to the module-level maintainer block. Two forcing functions live here: (1) the 'Stop. Don't execute.' close — dominant failure: student executes anyway; escape: Nerd line 'you've made the plan good; that IS the work.' (2) 'Save the rule only if it earned itself' — dominant failure: student skips compound when one DID earn itself; escape: Nerd: 'did any branch from the second read change how you'd read the next plan?'

rules_evaluated.evidence: Mood contract 'grounded competence' is honored throughout. The two-reads design pattern lands via doing (not lecture). Key Concepts breadcrumbs ('Assume 90% on first pass', 'Push-backs ship into memory') reinforce the mood. Bridge closes on the leverage horizon without premature resolution. Phase 4 trace mood_score 8 confirms landing.

No punitive framing. 'We all do it' normalizes rubber-stamping; the exercise frames the push-back as a skill to install, not a mistake to correct. Plan-mode approval inflation is named as a structural tell (RLHF), not a moral failure.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea leads with 'Reading a plan is finite' — a positive discipline framing. Failure mode ('plan-mode approval inflation') is the negative case the pairing defeats, named AFTER the student has already defeated it (at P5), not in the title.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check — no front-running downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M2 inlines the Socratic walk-down behavior as a plain prompt without naming Matt Pocock's grill-me skill. Bridge explicitly defers skill reveal to M3 ('Module 3 reveals that and has you author your first one'). strategy doc §M2: 'M3 owns first-Skill-use; M2 pre-installing a skill would front-run the teaching moment.' Confirmed consistent.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts surfaces: compounding ('Push-backs ship into memory' — Block 1 compounding theme); mirror ('the gap between them is where the plan-reading skill lives' — self-awareness theme); non-agentic-default ('Assumption-silent isn't assumption-free' — explicit assumption-making); 90% theme ('Assume 90% on first pass' breadcrumb). At least three themes covered per rule.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs, not standalone section.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bullets use tactical phrasing with bolded breadcrumbs: '**Push-backs ship into memory.**' and '**Assume 90% on first pass.**' Two breadcrumbs on the most load-bearing concepts. No standalone 'Themes this module instances:' section. Engineer register throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'When a plan is good' is placed before the exercise — it arms the student to do (shows ceiling of what a good plan looks like), consistent with the opener rule for demo/ceiling lectures. 'Where the rule could live' is placed after both exercises as a meta-frame closer that names three patterns the student already heard Claude propose. Placement is correct per the rule: opener = ceiling lecture, closer = meta-frame.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The main forcing functions are in fenced prompts: P2 prompt includes 'Plan the task I describe below. [...] Detail over brevity.'; P4 prompt includes 'one at a time'; P5 prompt enforces pattern-naming. The 'Don't execute the plan' instruction is in exercise section header and body — but the pattern-naming prompt at P5 is the actual forcing function that produces the teaching moment (it asks Claude to name the design pattern, which is the Evaluate beat). Body prose between prompts is explanatory, not load-bearing forcing functions. Compound prompt is fenced with correct carve-out label '*(Claude Code, only if something earned itself)*'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pattern-recognition LOs need n≥2 instances with contrast.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The 'Recognize the pairing as a repeatable design pattern' LO is met by two distinct reads (human push-back read + agent walk-down read) with explicit contrast at P5 ('what the second-pass read surfaced that my two push-backs didn't'). The exercise produces n=2 instances by design.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands — per phase + close (≥8 required; 7 = facilitator-premium; <7 = REVISE).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1 score: 7 (facilitator-premium — caused by 'room harvests everyone's' cue that reads as trainer-only noise in self-study; already flagged under rule 27). Phases 2-4 score: 8. Close score: 8. No phase below 7. Phase 1 at 7 is a facilitator-premium gap (the authored cue is the cause; fixing rule 27 likely brings Phase 1 to 8). No mood below 7 so not independently blocking, but the rule-27 fix will improve it.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Cutting the 'room harvests everyone's' sentence (rule 27 fix) likely lifts Phase 1 mood to 8.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M2 grounded competence must not preempt M3 skill-packaging.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bridge to M3: 'Module 3 reveals that and has you author your first one.' The second-pass read is inlined as a plain prompt — never named as a skill. The rules file in Extract the task-shaping rule is a .md, not a skill. The automation shapes are named but not built. Grounded competence closes without triggering M3's first-Skill-use teaching moment.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — plan-mode approval inflation catches reliably across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The teaching moment (approval inflation named after the student defeats it at P5) is structurally enforced: the P5 prompt asks Claude to name the design pattern and compare the two reads. Even a fast operator who skims body prose reaches P5 because it's a fenced prompt. Even a senior engineer who genuinely reads plans carefully encounters 'softening-on-regeneration' at P3 as a teaching moment. The pattern lands across skill variation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — module honors the Big Idea framing rather than a different frame.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea: 'Reading a plan is finite. Your own read catches some of it, a second agent walking the decision tree catches the rest.' The module opens with the two-reads premise, delivers both reads as exercises, closes with the gap analysis at P5. The 'plan-mode approval inflation' label names the failure mode the pairing defeats — consistent with strategy doc framing 'what M2 refuses: plan mode as a feature tour.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — M2 close hands off to M3 open correctly.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bridge: 'Module 3 takes the same move into durable infrastructure: the judges and gates you ship to your team kit catch exactly the kind of branch the second read surfaced today, before a teammate needs to ask. And the move itself turns out to be packageable as a skill; Module 3 reveals that and has you author your first one.' Strategy doc M3 = 'Earn the trust (Q+S, team kit BIRTH).' Handoff is accurate: the push-back discipline becomes the gate spec at M3. Skill reveal is deferred to M3 correctly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Between-module reading appears in two places.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Pre-reads before M3 appear in M2 body (lines 61-67: Willison lethal trifecta + OWASP). M1 (getting-going.md) has '## Homework after Module 1, between-module reading' with Cherny + survival guide, and M2's Prework section references both. The M2→M3 pre-reads need to also appear in M3's Prework section — this is a technical-class check (not story-class primary) but noted for cross-class awareness.

stealing_the_mood: Phase 1 (Connections): 'The room harvests everyone's before the lecture names the pattern' — cohort-management cue in student-facing body creates self-study confusion and reads as trainer instruction. Causes 7 instead of 8. Fix: cut the sentence (already flagged blocking under rule 27).
